title: A name I always forget
summary: Reflect on the strange feeling of forgetting one person's name, and what you remember about them instead.
topic: Forgetting one person's name but remembering other things about them
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a person whose name you can never remember. Say who they are, where you see them, and what you do remember about them instead. Reflect on why a name is hard to keep but a face or a voice is easy. Use present simple, past simple, and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: There is a person I see often, but I always forget…
I remember his / her face and… but the name just…
It is strange because I never forget…
sampleText: There is a woman in my building who I see almost every day. She lives on the third floor. She always says hello to me at the door. But I always forget her name. It is very strange. I remember her face and her kind smile. I remember her small grey dog and her green umbrella. I remember her soft voice. But the name just goes away. Last month she told me her name again. I said it three times in my head. The next morning I forgot it again. Now I feel a little embarrassed. I never ask her again because it is not polite the fourth time. So I just smile and say hello. My memory keeps the picture but loses the word. I think a face is easy because I see it many times. A name is only a sound, and I hear it once. I am not a bad neighbour. I remember everything except her name. One day I will write it down and keep it safe.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi luôn quên tên cô ấy
nụ cười tử tế của cô ấy
chiếc ô màu xanh lá của cô ấy
cái tên cứ trôi đi mất

sampleAnnotations.phrase: keeps the picture but loses the word
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: giữ lại hình ảnh nhưng đánh mất từ ngữ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: A name is only a sound
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một cái tên chỉ là một âm thanh

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency before the main verb
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Các trạng từ chỉ tần suất như 'always' và 'never' đứng trước động từ chính: 'I always forget her name', 'I never ask her again'. Nhưng với động từ 'be' hay 'says', vị trí có thể khác. Hãy chú ý thứ tự từ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Adverbs of frequency like 'always' and 'never' go before the main verb: 'I always forget her name', 'I never ask her again'. Watch the word order so the adverb sits in the right place.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một người phụ nữ trong tòa nhà của tôi mà tôi gặp gần như mỗi ngày. Cô ấy sống ở tầng ba. Cô ấy luôn chào tôi ở cửa. Nhưng tôi luôn quên tên cô ấy. Thật kỳ lạ. Tôi nhớ khuôn mặt và nụ cười tử tế của cô ấy. Tôi nhớ con chó nhỏ màu xám và chiếc ô màu xanh lá của cô ấy. Tôi nhớ giọng nói nhẹ nhàng của cô ấy. Nhưng cái tên thì cứ trôi đi mất. Tháng trước cô ấy nói tên cho tôi lần nữa. Tôi nhắc lại nó ba lần trong đầu. Sáng hôm sau tôi lại quên. Bây giờ tôi cảm thấy hơi ngượng. Tôi không bao giờ hỏi cô ấy lại vì hỏi lần thứ tư thì không lịch sự. Nên tôi chỉ mỉm cười và chào. Trí nhớ của tôi giữ lại hình ảnh nhưng đánh mất từ ngữ. Tôi nghĩ một khuôn mặt thì dễ vì tôi nhìn nó nhiều lần. Một cái tên chỉ là một âm thanh, và tôi nghe nó một lần. Tôi không phải là một hàng xóm tệ. Tôi nhớ mọi thứ trừ cái tên của cô ấy. Một ngày nào đó tôi sẽ viết nó ra và giữ cho an toàn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I forget always her name.
I always forget her name.
Always I forget her name name.
I always forgetting her name.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb: 'I always forget'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go with a main verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'She ___ hello to me at the door.'
options: say
saying
says
sayed
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'she' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for last month?
options: Last month she telled me her name again.
Last month she tell me her name again.
Last month she is telling me her name again.
Last month she told me her name again.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'told' is the irregular past simple of 'tell'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show the contrast?
options: I remember her face, but I forget her name.
I remember her face, but her name I forget forget.
I remember her face but her name I.
But I remember her face I forget her name.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses, each with subject + verb + object.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why you do not ask her name again?
options: I never ask her again because not polite fourth time.
I never ask her again because it is not polite the fourth time.
I never ask her again so the fourth time polite.
Because not polite I her again never ask.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full clause; 'never' is correctly before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural about your feeling?
options: Now I feel a little embarrassing.
Now I am feel a little embarrassed.
Now I feel a little embarrassed.
Now I feels a little embarrassed.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I feel embarrassed' uses the correct -ed adjective for how you feel.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'My memory ___ the picture.'
options: keep
keeping
kept
keeps
mcQuestions.explanation: 'memory' is third-person singular, so the verb takes -s: 'keeps'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural reflection on a name?
options: A name is only a sound, and I hear it once.
A name is only a sound, and I hears it once.
A name are only a sound, and I hear it once.
A name is only a sound, and I hear it once download.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A name is' (singular) and 'I hear' (no -s) are both correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb after 'A name' and after 'I'.

options: I remember everything except her name forgets.
I remember everything except her name.
I remember everythings except her name.
I remember everything excepting her name is.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'everything except her name' is correct and natural.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option keeps 'everything' and 'except her name' simple and correct?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural future plan?
options: One day I will writes it down and keep it safe.
One day I will to write it down and keep it safe.
One day I will write it down and keep it safe.
One day I will write it down and keep it safely place.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'will write' and 'keep' (base verbs after 'will') are correct and natural.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think we can remember a person's face or voice for years, but forget their name after only a few minutes?
